channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Labour MPs challenge proposed U.K. immigration reforms amid internal party disagreement
item.description: Over 100 Labour Party MPs have written to Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood urging a reconsideration of proposed changes to the U.K.’s immigration system. The reforms would make it harder for low-income ...

item.title: Jane Gordon Shares Personal Journey of Stroke Recovery in Her 60s
item.description: In October, Jane Gordon, a journalist and author in her late 60s, experienced an unexpected stroke, despite leading an active life and feeling mentally agile. Her day involved caring for elderly clien...

item.title: Nitish Kumar likely to shift to Rajya Sabha as BJP prepares to appoint its first Chief Minister in Bihar
item.description: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar is reportedly preparing to move to the Rajya Sabha, a development that could lead to the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) appointing its own Chief Minister in the state f...

item.title: PAA Clarifies Pakistan's Airspace Fully Open, Rejects Reports of Partial Closure Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: The Pakistan Airports Authority (PAA) has strongly denied media reports claiming partial closure of Pakistan's airspace for commercial flights due to security concerns from the US-Iran conflict. In an...

item.title: Countries boycott Milan Cortina Paralympics opening ceremony amid geopolitical tensions
item.description: The 2026 Winter Paralympics opening ceremony in Milan Cortina, scheduled for Friday, faces boycotts from several countries due to ongoing geopolitical tensions. Nations including the Czech Republic, F...

item.title: Merseyside Police conduct large-scale operation in Liverpool targeting suspected drug supply
item.description: In the early hours in Liverpool's Anfield area, hundreds of police officers carried out Operation Vanguard, a coordinated campaign targeting properties suspected of involvement in drug supply. Officer...

item.title: Over 200 Australians return home after being stranded in Dubai amid Middle East tensions
item.description: More than 200 Australians have safely returned to Sydney after being stranded in Dubai for several days due to escalating conflict in the Middle East. The disruption followed US and Israeli strikes on...

item.title: Stakeholders Urge Strong Measures to Protect Artists and Formalize Creative Work in Tanzania
item.description: Stakeholders in Tanzania's arts and creative sector are urging the government and relevant authorities to take urgent steps to ensure decent work conditions for artists. During a three-day workshop in...

item.title: Police shut down illegal detergent manufacturing operation in Villa Liza
item.description: Police in Ekurhuleni have shut down an illegal detergent factory operating out of a shack in the Snake Park section of Villa Liza. The operation, uncovered shortly after a similar bust in nearby Windm...

item.title: Scheduled Kenya Power Maintenance to Cause Temporary Power Outages Across Multiple Counties
item.description: Kenya Power and Lighting Company (KPLC) has announced planned electricity interruptions across several counties on Thursday, March 5, as part of scheduled maintenance works aimed at improving power re...

item.title: Court overturns life sentence due to legal irregularities in child abuse case
item.description: Arusha – The Court of Appeal has released a father who had been sentenced to life imprisonment for allegedly sodomising his four-year-old son. The court found serious legal flaws in the prosecution’s ...

item.title: Karachi Faces Third Day of Road Closures Due to Security Concerns After Protests Over Khamenei Assassination
item.description: Several important roads in Karachi, including Mai Kolachi Road, PIDC Bridge, and routes connected to Jinnah Bridge, stayed closed on Wednesday for the third day running because of increased security m...

item.title: SANDF to assist SAPS in combating organised crime from 31 March
item.description: The South African National Defence Force (SANDF) will begin supporting the South African Police Service (SAPS) in tackling organised crime from 31 March 2026 for a year, ending on 31 March 2027. Natio...

item.title: Kidnappers Demand N10 Million Ransom for Six Celestial Church Members Abducted in Ondo, Threaten Execution by Monday Afternoon
item.description: Gunmen abducted six members of the Celestial Church of Christ, Jordan Parish, located in Uso community, Owo Local Government Area of Ondo State, during an early morning prayer service last Wednesday. ...

item.title: Analysing the DRC Crisis: Governance, Persecution, and Western Influence
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) remains a focal point of conflict in the Great Lakes region, with the crisis deeply rooted in poor governance, systemic persecution of Kinyarwanda-speaking Congo...

item.title: Nigeria Reopens Kebbi Borders, Trucks Resume Movement to Niger and Benin
item.description: Nigeria don officially reopen her borders with Niger and Benin, following months of closure wey affect cross-border trade and economy. The Kamba and Tsamiya border posts for Kebbi state don dey operat...

item.title: Hezbollah’s actions continue to threaten Lebanon’s stability amid regional tensions
item.description: In early 2026, Lebanon faces renewed instability following U.S.-Israeli strikes in Iran and the assassination of Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Hezbollah, a Shiite paramilitary group a...

item.title: MOI Clarifies Amputation Procedures Are Only Life-Saving
item.description: Dodoma — The Muhimbili Orthopaedic Institute (MOI) has cleared the air after rumours say doctors deliberately cut off limbs of accident victims. Ambassador Dr Mpoki Ulisubisya, Executive Director of M...

item.title: US-Israel Conflict in Iran Moves to Attrition with Economic and Regional Impacts
item.description: On the fifth day of the US-Israel war against Iran, the conflict has entered a phase of attrition rather than decisive military victory. The coalition has gained tactical air superiority, significantl...

item.title: Authorities Investigate Hazing Death of Maritime Student in Cavite
item.description: Authorities in Cavite are investigating the death of 19-year-old freshman maritime student Mark Kenneth Alcedo, who reportedly died due to severe hazing. Police identified 21 individuals as involved, ...
